headline: Here's How Much $1000 Invested In Lam Research 20 Years Ago Would Be Worth Today
summary: Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) has outperformed the market over the past 20 years by 13.78% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 23.04%. Currently, Lam Research has a market capitalization of $370.03 billion. Buying $1000 In LRCX: If an investor had bought $1000 of LRCX stock 20 years ago, it would be worth $62,148.71 today based on a price of $295.89 for LRCX at the time of writing. headline: Is Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
summary: Is LRCX a good stock to buy? We came across a bullish thesis on Lam Research Corporation on Nikhs’s Substack. In this article, we will summarize the bulls’ thesis on LRCX. Lam Research Corporation’s share was trading at $258.57 as of May 4th. LRCX’s trailing and forward P/E were 48.53 and 33.22 respectively according to Yahoo Finance. […]

headline: SanDisk And Micron Had Their Run – This New 'Photonics' ETF Has 15 Triple-Digit Gainers Inside

headline: How to Beat the S&P 500 With a Dividend ETF: Give Up the Dividend.
summary: First Trust Rising Dividend Achievers has returned an annual 15.8% over the past 10 years. But don’t look too closely at its dividend.
